page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the D8s_v7 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core CPU performance. Its single-threaded compression and decompression performance is highly efficient, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scores reaching the top 10% tier. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including large-block read and write operations, fall within the average range. Database operations, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations also align with average tier performance. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves strong text generation speeds on small 135M parameter LLM models, though prompt processing and larger 7B model inference remain average.

From a resource density perspective, the D8s_v7 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio without the overhead of local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed provide stable compute performance, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for applications that do not require massive parallelization or GPU acceleration. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.

Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression speeds, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v7 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, suited for web serving and database workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, balanced memory allocation, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 ratio
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at a high 4.2 GHz clock speed
Top-tier single-threaded compression and strong single-core performance
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX hardware acceleration features
Premium Storage capable with no local storage bundled
Suited for web serving, database operations, and lightweight inference
